name: Monte da Lebre y Monte Mayor - circular from Foz
name_original: Monte da Lebre y Monte Mayor - circular desde Foz
region_intro: Jogging routes around Foz are characterized by a diverse landscape that includes coastal paths, sandy beaches, and inland trails. The region features varied terrain, from flat promenades ideal for easy runs to more challenging routes with moderate elevation gains into the surrounding hills. Runners can experience both the Atlantic coastline and the natural beauty of the interior. The area provides a mix of environments suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Foz The most popular jogging route is Monte da Lebre y Monte Mayor - circular from Foz, a 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 46 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through inland hills. Another top favourite among local runners is Coastal Walkway of Foz – A Rapadoira Beach loop from Foz, a moderate 8.4 miles (13.5 km) path. This route offers scenic views along the Foz coastline and beaches. Local runners also love the Praia de Tupide loop from Foz, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and beaches, often completed in about 41 minutes. Jogging around Foz is defined by coastal paths, beaches, and inland hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal runs to more demanding hill climbs. The routes in Foz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from a small number of reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Foz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Foz?
A: There are over 25 running routes around Foz, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include coastal paths, sandy beaches, and trails through inland hills, catering to various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Foz?
A: Yes, Foz offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners. The region features flat promenades and coastal paths that are ideal for a relaxed run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24610821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia de Tupide loop from Foz&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.0-mile (6.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and beaches, often completed in about 41 minutes, making it a good option for those starting out.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on runs around Foz?
A: Running routes in Foz offer a rich variety of scenery. You can expect stunning coastal views, runs along sandy beaches, and trails that venture into the natural beauty of inland hills. The region's landscape provides a mix of environments, from the Atlantic coastline to more wooded areas, ensuring a scenic experience for every runner.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Foz?
A: Yes, many running routes in Foz are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991003359/monte-da-lebre-y-monte-mayor-circular-from-foz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte da Lebre y Monte Mayor - circular from Foz&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.5-mile (13.7 km) trail that takes you through inland hills. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24613287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Walkway of Foz – A Rapadoira Beach loop from Foz&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views along the coastline.

Q: Can I combine my run with visiting natural attractions or landmarks?
A: Absolutely! Foz offers routes that pass by or are close to beautiful natural attractions. You can run along the coast and discover highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/526099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2833444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia das Illas&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3163847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de los Castros Natural Rock Arch&lt;/a&gt; is another impressive natural monument you might encounter on coastal trails.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Foz?
A: The running routes in Foz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.5 stars. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from easy coastal runs to more demanding hill climbs, and the stunning views offered along the Atlantic coastline and beaches. The variety ensures there's something for every ability level.

Q: Are there running paths suitable for families?
A: While the guide focuses on jogging, many of the coastal paths and promenades in Foz are suitable for families looking for a leisurely walk or light jog. Urban green spaces like Praça Arborizada e Pista de Caminhada or Gramadão are also excellent choices for family-friendly outdoor activity, offering serene paths and lush greenery.

Q: Are there any routes where I might spot wildlife?
A: The natural areas around Foz, particularly those venturing into the Atlantic Forest, offer opportunities for wildlife spotting. While the guide's routes are primarily for jogging, trails like the Poço Preto Trail and Banana Tree Trail (within the Iguaçu National Park, as per regional research) are known for birdwatching and nature immersion, suggesting that similar natural environments in Foz could offer glimpses of local fauna.

Q: What are some more challenging running routes in the Foz area?
A: For runners seeking a challenge, Foz offers routes with significant elevation gain through inland hills. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991003359/monte-da-lebre-y-monte-mayor-circular-from-foz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte da Lebre y Monte Mayor - circular from Foz&lt;/a&gt; is classified as 'difficult', covering 8.5 miles (13.7 km) with substantial uphill sections, making it a great option for experienced runners.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of significant landmarks?
A: Yes, several routes provide views of significant landmarks. Beyond natural monuments, you might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Salvador&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy scenic vistas of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4755816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ría de Foz&lt;/a&gt;. The coastal paths often offer glimpses of the Foz Harbor and other points of interest.

Q: Are there options for running in urban green spaces in Foz?
A: Yes, for those preferring runs closer to the city, Foz provides urban green spaces. Praça Arborizada e Pista de Caminhada offers serene walking paths, and the running track on Av. Paraná (around 4.5 km) or routes around CTG Charrua (around 5 km) are popular urban choices. Gramadão is another park suitable for exercise within the city.

Q: Are there any coastal running routes with direct beach access?
A: Foz is renowned for its coastal paths and beaches, and many running routes offer direct access or run alongside them. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24613287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Walkway of Foz – A Rapadoira Beach loop from Foz&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing scenic views and opportunities to run right by the sand.
tips:
Romanesque-style basilica located on the outskirts of the coastal town of Foz, considered the oldest cathedral in Spain.
Next to the temple is the fountain of A Zapata. According to legend, Bishop San Gonzalo dropped a slipper and water gushed out. It is for this reason that there is a belief that the source has miraculous properties.

waypoints.name: Basilica of San Martiño de Mondoñedo
